revision.text: #redirect[[en:Tenshi-tsukinuke]] [[Category:Automatic wikipedia redirects]]

{{Pathnav|日本|近畿地方|京都府|京都市|下京区|frame=1}} {{Infobox settlement | name = 天使突抜 | settlement_type = [[町・字]] | image_skyline = 2016 0620 Kyoto Tenshi-tsukinuke Plate.jpg | imagesize = 200px | image_caption = | image_map = | map_caption = | pushpin_map = Japan Kyoto | latd = 34 | latm = 59 | lats = 54.2 | latNS = N | longd = 135 | longm = 45 | longs = 15.8 | longEW = E | coordinates_type = region:JP-26_scale:5000 | coordinates_display = inline,title | subdivision_type = [[国の一覧|国]] | subdivision_name = {{JPN}} | subdivision_type1 = [[都道府県]] | subdivision_name1 = [[File:Flag of Kyoto Prefecture.svg|border|25px|京都府旗]] [[京都府]] | subdivision_type2 = [[市町村]] | subdivision_name2 = [[File:Flag of Kyoto City.svg|border|25px|京都市旗]] [[京都市]] | subdivision_type3 = [[区 (行政区画)|区]] | subdivision_name3 = [[下京区]] | subdivision_type4 = [[町・字|町]] | subdivision_name4 = 天使突抜 | established_title1 = <!--- 制定・制定(<ref>) ---> | established_date1 = <!--- 制定日 ---> | area_footnotes = | area_total_km2 = | elevation_footnotes = <!--- 標高(<ref>) ---> | elevation_m = <!--- 標高 ---> | population_as_of= | population_footnotes = | population_total = | population_density_km2 = auto | timezone1 = [[日本標準時]] | utc_offset1 = +9 | postal_code_type = [[日本の郵便番号|郵便番号]] | postal_code = 600-8456<ref name="postal">{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.post.japanpost.jp/cgi-zip/zipcode.php?pref=26&city=1261060&id=102341|title=郵便番号|publisher=[[日本郵便]] |accessdate=2024-07-28}}</ref> | area_code = | registration_plate = [[京都運輸支局|京都]] | footnotes = }} '''天使突抜'''（てんしつきぬけ）は、[[京都府]][[京都市]][[下京区]]の町名。南北に走る[[東中筋通]]を挟む両側町で、[[松原通]]から[[六条通]]までの間に位置する{{sfn|角川日本地名大辞典編纂委員会|1982|pp=}}{{sfn|糸井通浩|2013|p=338}}。名称はかつて天使社（てんししゃ）を号した[[五條天神社 (京都市)|五条天神社]]の境内を貫通して造られた天使突抜通（現東中筋通）に因む{{sfn|角川日本地名大辞典編纂委員会|1982|pp=}}{{sfn|糸井通浩|2013|p=338}}。
天使突抜は、北は天神前町に接し、そこから南へ1丁目（[[万寿寺通]]まで）、2丁目（[[五条通]]まで）、3丁目（[[楊梅通]]まで）、4丁目（六条通まで）の順に並ぶ{{sfn|源城政好|下坂守|2005|p=109}}。
天正18年（1590年）に豊臣秀吉が京都改造（[[天正の地割]]）を行った際、五条天神社の境内に新しく街路を通した。五条天神社は天使社とも呼ばれたことから、この街路は天使突抜通と称された{{sfn|源城政好|下坂守|2005|p=109}}。『京都坊目誌』（1915年）によれば、天使突抜通は当初は[[高辻通]]から[[仏光寺通]]までであったが、のちに塩小路通まで伸延した{{sfn|糸井通浩|2013|p=338}}。
天使突抜は、『[[寛永]]十四年洛中絵図』（1637年）では「天使」と記されていたが、『京雀』（1665年）では「天子（ママ）のつきぬけ」と記され、『京町鏡』（1762年）に至り現在と同じ「天使突抜」と記されるようになった{{sfn|源城政好|下坂守|2005|p=109}}。なお『京雀』では天使突抜4丁目を「しのむら町」と記すが、由来は不明である{{sfn|角川日本地名大辞典編纂委員会|1982|pp=}}{{sfn|源城政好|下坂守|2005|p=109}}。
江戸期は、1・2丁目が川西九町組卜味三町組、3丁目が川西九町和泉組、4丁目は川西九町中筋組に属した。明治2年（1869年）の町組改正により13番組となり、第17区、第17学区を経て、明治12年に下京区に編入された{{sfn|角川日本地名大辞典編纂委員会|1982|pp=}}。
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} === 出典 === <!-- 文献参照ページ --> {{Reflist|20em}}
== 参考文献 == * {{Cite book|和書|author=角川日本地名大辞典編纂委員会 |title=角川日本地名大辞典 |volume=26-1 京都府 上巻 |year=1982 |publisher=[[角川書店]] |isbn= |ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|和書|author=糸井通浩 |author-link=糸井通浩 |title=京都地名語源辞典 |editor=吉田金彦|editor-link=吉田金彦|editor2=糸井通浩|editor3=綱本逸雄|year=2013 |publisher=[[東京堂出版]] |isbn=978-4-490-10841-5 |ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|和書|title=京都の地名由来辞典 |editor=源城政好|editor2=下坂守|editor2-link=下坂守|year=2005 |publisher=東京堂出版 |isbn=4-490-10683-1 |ref=harv}}
== 関連項目 == * [[通崎睦美]] - 天使突抜に在住。随筆『天使突抜一丁目-着物と自転車と』などを執筆 * [[吉村達也]] - 小説家。『京都天使突抜通の恋』を執筆
{{デフォルトソート:てんしつきぬけ}} [[Category:下京区の町・字]]

revision.text:
{{Pathnav|日本|近畿地方|京都府|京都市|下京区|frame=1}} {{Infobox settlement | name = 天使突抜 | settlement_type = [[町・字]] | image_skyline = 2016 0620 Kyoto Tenshi-tsukinuke Plate.jpg | imagesize = 200px | image_caption = | image_map = | map_caption = | pushpin_map = Japan Kyoto city | latd = 34 | latm = 59 | lats = 54.2 | latNS = N | longd = 135 | longm = 45 | longs = 15.8 | longEW = E | coordinates_type = region:JP-26_scale:5000 | coordinates_display = inline,title | subdivision_type = [[国の一覧|国]] | subdivision_name = {{JPN}} | subdivision_type1 = [[都道府県]] | subdivision_name1 = [[File:Flag of Kyoto Prefecture.svg|border|25px|京都府旗]] [[京都府]] | subdivision_type2 = [[市町村]] | subdivision_name2 = [[File:Flag of Kyoto City.svg|border|25px|京都市旗]] [[京都市]] | subdivision_type3 = [[区 (行政区画)|区]] | subdivision_name3 = [[下京区]] | subdivision_type4 = [[町・字|町]] | subdivision_name4 = 天使突抜 | established_title1 = <!--- 制定・制定(<ref>) ---> | established_date1 = <!--- 制定日 ---> | area_footnotes = | area_total_km2 = | elevation_footnotes = <!--- 標高(<ref>) ---> | elevation_m = <!--- 標高 ---> | population_as_of= | population_footnotes = | population_total = | population_density_km2 = auto | timezone1 = [[日本標準時]] | utc_offset1 = +9 | postal_code_type = [[日本の郵便番号|郵便番号]] | postal_code = 600-8456<ref name="postal">{{Cite web|和書|url=https://www.post.japanpost.jp/cgi-zip/zipcode.php?pref=26&city=1261060&id=102341|title=郵便番号|publisher=[[日本郵便]] |accessdate=2024-07-28}}</ref> | area_code = | registration_plate = [[京都運輸支局|京都]] | footnotes = }} '''天使突抜'''（てんしつきぬけ）は、[[京都府]][[京都市]][[下京区]]の町名。南北に走る[[東中筋通]]を挟む両側町で、[[松原通 (京都市)|松原通]]から[[六条通]]までの間に位置する{{sfn|角川日本地名大辞典編纂委員会|1982|pp=}}{{sfn|糸井通浩|2013|p=338}}。名称はかつて天使社（てんししゃ）を号した[[五條天神社 (京都市)|五条天神社]]の境内を貫通して造られた天使突抜通（現東中筋通）に因む{{sfn|角川日本地名大辞典編纂委員会|1982|pp=}}{{sfn|糸井通浩|2013|p=338}}。
天使突抜は、北は天神前町に接し、そこから南へ1丁目（[[万寿寺通]]まで）、2丁目（[[五条通]]まで）、3丁目（[[楊梅通]]まで）、4丁目（六条通まで）の順に並ぶ{{sfn|源城政好|下坂守|2005|p=109}}。
天正18年（1590年）に豊臣秀吉が京都改造（[[天正の地割]]）を行った際、五条天神社の境内に新しく街路を通した。五条天神社は天使社とも呼ばれたことから、この街路は天使突抜通と称された{{sfn|源城政好|下坂守|2005|p=109}}。『京都坊目誌』（1915年）によれば、天使突抜通は当初は[[高辻通]]から[[仏光寺通]]までであったが、のちに塩小路通まで伸延した{{sfn|糸井通浩|2013|p=338}}。
天使突抜は、『[[寛永]]十四年洛中絵図』（1637年）では「天使」と記されていたが、『京雀』（1665年）では「天子（ママ）のつきぬけ」と記され、『京町鏡』（1762年）に至り現在と同じ「天使突抜」と記されるようになった{{sfn|源城政好|下坂守|2005|p=109}}。なお『京雀』では天使突抜4丁目を「しのむら町」と記すが、由来は不明である{{sfn|角川日本地名大辞典編纂委員会|1982|pp=}}{{sfn|源城政好|下坂守|2005|p=109}}。
江戸期は、1・2丁目が川西九町組卜味三町組、3丁目が川西九町和泉組、4丁目は川西九町中筋組に属した。明治2年（1869年）の町組改正により13番組となり、第17区、第17学区を経て、明治12年に下京区に編入された{{sfn|角川日本地名大辞典編纂委員会|1982|pp=}}。
== 脚注 == {{脚注ヘルプ}} === 出典 === <!-- 文献参照ページ --> {{Reflist|20em}}
== 参考文献 == * {{Cite book|和書|author=角川日本地名大辞典編纂委員会 |title=角川日本地名大辞典 |volume=26-1 京都府 上巻 |year=1982 |publisher=[[角川書店]] |isbn= |ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|和書|author=糸井通浩 |author-link=糸井通浩 |title=京都地名語源辞典 |editor=吉田金彦|editor-link=吉田金彦|editor2=糸井通浩|editor3=綱本逸雄|year=2013 |publisher=[[東京堂出版]] |isbn=978-4-490-10841-5 |ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|和書|title=京都の地名由来辞典 |editor=源城政好|editor2=下坂守|editor2-link=下坂守|year=2005 |publisher=東京堂出版 |isbn=4-490-10683-1 |ref=harv}}
== 関連項目 == * [[通崎睦美]] - 天使突抜に在住。随筆『天使突抜一丁目-着物と自転車と』などを執筆 * [[吉村達也]] - 小説家。『京都天使突抜通の恋』を執筆 * [[山田雅史]] - 映画監督。『天使突抜六丁目』を製作
{{デフォルトソート:てんしつきぬけ}} [[Category:下京区の町・字]]

wikidata|WD=Q11375707}} | footnotes = }}
'''Tenshitsukinuke''' (天使突抜) is a town name located in [[:en:Shimogyō-ku, Kyoto|Shimogyō Ward]], [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto City]], [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]]. It spans both sides of the {{ill|Higashinakasuji Street|ja|東中筋通|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashinakasuji Street|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 小川通|1=Ogawa Street|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11460614}}, running north-south between {{ill|Matsubara Street (Kyoto)|ja|松原通 (京都市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsubara Street (Kyoto)|qq=|1=Matsubara Street|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11529460}} and {{ill|Rokujō Street|ja|六条通|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokujō Street|qq=|1=Rokujō Street|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11392417}}. The name originates from the Tenshi Shrine (天使社), a former designation of {{ill|Gojō Tenjin Shrine (Kyoto City)|ja|五條天神社 (京都市)|1=Gojō Tenjin-gū|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gojō Tenjin Shrine (Kyoto City)|13=User:Immanuelle/Gojō Tenjin-gū|qq=}}, through which the Tenshitsukinuke Street (now Higashinakasuji Street) was constructed.
The area is bordered to the north by Tenjinmae-chō and consists of four sections arranged southward: 1-chōme (up to {{ill|Manjuji Street|ja|万寿寺通|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manjuji Street|qq=|1=Manjuji Street|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11353816}}), 2-chōme (up to [[:en:Gojō Street|Gojō Street]]), 3-chōme (up to {{ill|Yōbai Street|ja|楊梅通|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yōbai Street|qq=|1=Yōbai Street|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24887425}}), and 4-chōme (up to Rokujō Street).
In the 18th year of the Tenshō era (1590), [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Toyotomi Hideyoshi]] implemented a redesign of Kyoto, known as the {{ill|Tenshō Land Division|ja|天正の地割|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenshō Land Division|qq=|1=Tenshō Land Division|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11442707}}, constructing a new street through the precincts of Gojō Tenjin Shrine. As the shrine was also called Tenshi Shrine, this street was named Tenshitsukinuke Street. According to the 1915 ''Kyōto Bōmeishi,'' Tenshitsukinuke Street initially extended from {{ill|Takatsuji Street|ja|高辻通|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takatsuji Street|qq=|1=Takatsuji Street|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11672896}} to {{ill|Bukkoji Street|ja|仏光寺通|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bukkoji Street|qq=|1=Bukkōji Street|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11377503}}, later being extended to {{ill|Shio-kōji Street|ja|塩小路通|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shio-kōji Street|qq=|1=Shiokōji Street|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11428656}}.
During the Edo period, 1-chōme and 2-chōme belonged to Kawanishi Ku-chōgumi Tomi San-chōgumi, 3-chōme to Kawanishi Ku-chō Izumi-gumi, and 4-chōme to Kawanishi Ku-chō Nakasuji-gumi. In the Meiji era, the area underwent administrative changes, becoming part of the 13th group, the 17th district, and eventually incorporated into Shimogyō Ward in 1879.
== Notable residents and works == * {{ill|Mutsumi Tsūzaki|ja|通崎睦美|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mutsumi Tsūzaki|qq=|1=Mutsumi Tsūzaki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11639574}} - A resident of Tenshitsukinuke who authored essays like ''Tenshitsukinuke 1-Chōme: Kimono and Bicycles.'' * {{ill|Tatsuya Yoshimura|ja|吉村達也|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tatsuya Yoshimura|qq=|1=Tatsuya Yoshimura|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11413314}} - A novelist who wrote ''Kyoto Tenshitsukinuke-dōri no Koi'' (Kyoto's Love on Tenshitsukinuke Street). * {{ill|Masashi Yamada (director)|ja|山田雅史|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masashi Yamada (director)|qq=|1=Masafumi Yamada|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11470732}} - A film director who produced ''Tenshitsukinuke Rokuchōme.''

== Notable residents and works == * {{ill|Mutsumi Tsūzaki|ja|通崎睦美|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mutsumi Tsūzaki|1=Mutsumi Tsūzaki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11639574}} - A resident of Tenshitsukinuke who authored essays like ''Tenshitsukinuke 1-Chōme: Kimono and Bicycles.'' * {{ill|Tatsuya Yoshimura|ja|吉村達也|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tatsuya Yoshimura|1=Tatsuya Yoshimura|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11413314}} - A novelist who wrote ''Kyoto Tenshitsukinuke-dōri no Koi'' (Kyoto's Love on Tenshitsukinuke Street). * {{ill|Masashi Yamada (director)|ja|山田雅史|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masashi Yamada (director)|1=Masafumi Yamada|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11470732}} - A film director who produced ''Tenshitsukinuke Rokuchōme.''

'''Tenshitsukinuke''' (天使突抜) is a town name located in [[:en:Shimogyō-ku, Kyoto|Shimogyō Ward]], [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto City]], [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]]. It spans both sides of the {{ill|Higashinakasuji Street|ja|東中筋通|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashinakasuji Street|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 小川通|1=Ogawa Street|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11460614}}, running north-south between {{ill|Matsubara Street (Kyoto)|ja|松原通 (京都市)|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsubara Street (Kyoto)|1=Matsubara Street|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11529460}} and {{ill|Rokujō Street|ja|六条通|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokujō Street|1=Rokujō Street|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11392417}}. The name originates from the Tenshi Shrine (天使社), a former designation of {{ill|Gojō Tenjin Shrine (Kyoto City)|ja|五條天神社 (京都市)|1=Gojō Tenjin-gū|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gojō Tenjin Shrine (Kyoto City)|13=User:Immanuelle/Gojō Tenjin-gū|qq=}}, through which the Tenshitsukinuke Street (now Higashinakasuji Street) was constructed.
The area is bordered to the north by Tenjinmae-chō and consists of four sections arranged southward: 1-chōme (up to {{ill|Manjuji Street|ja|万寿寺通|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manjuji Street|1=Manjuji Street|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11353816}}), 2-chōme (up to [[:en:Gojō Street|Gojō Street]]), 3-chōme (up to {{ill|Yōbai Street|ja|楊梅通|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yōbai Street|1=Yōbai Street|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24887425}}), and 4-chōme (up to Rokujō Street).
In the 18th year of the Tenshō era (1590), [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Toyotomi Hideyoshi]] implemented a redesign of Kyoto, known as the {{ill|Tenshō Land Division|ja|天正の地割|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenshō Land Division|1=Tenshō Land Division|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11442707}}, constructing a new street through the precincts of Gojō Tenjin Shrine. As the shrine was also called Tenshi Shrine, this street was named Tenshitsukinuke Street. According to the 1915 ''Kyōto Bōmeishi,'' Tenshitsukinuke Street initially extended from {{ill|Takatsuji Street|ja|高辻通|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takatsuji Street|1=Takatsuji Street|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11672896}} to {{ill|Bukkoji Street|ja|仏光寺通|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bukkoji Street|1=Bukkōji Street|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11377503}}, later being extended to {{ill|Shio-kōji Street|ja|塩小路通|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shio-kōji Street|1=Shiokōji Street|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11428656}}.
During the Edo period, 1-chōme and 2-chōme belonged to Kawanishi Ku-chōgumi Tomi San-chōgumi, 3-chōme to Kawanishi Ku-chō Izumi-gumi, and 4-chōme to Kawanishi Ku-chō Nakasuji-gumi. In the Meiji era, the area underwent administrative changes, becoming part of the 13th group, the 17th district, and eventually incorporated into Shimogyō Ward in 1879.
== Notable residents and works == * {{ill|Mutsumi Tsūzaki|ja|通崎睦美|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mutsumi Tsūzaki|1=Mutsumi Tsūzaki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11639574}} - A resident of Tenshitsukinuke who authored essays like ''Tenshitsukinuke 1-Chōme: Kimono and Bicycles.'' * {{ill|Tatsuya Yoshimura|ja|吉村達也|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tatsuya Yoshimura|1=Tatsuya Yoshimura|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11413314}} - A novelist who wrote ''Kyoto Tenshitsukinuke-dōri no Koi'' (Kyoto's Love on Tenshitsukinuke Street). * {{ill|Masashi Yamada (director)|ja|山田雅史|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masashi Yamada (director)|1=Masafumi Yamada|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11470732}} - A film director who produced ''Tenshitsukinuke Rokuchōme.''

web|和書|url=https://www.post.japanpost.jp/cgi-zip/zipcode.php?pref=26&city=1261060&id=102341|title=郵便番号|publisher=[[:en:Japan Post Service|Japan Post]] |accessdate=2024-07-28}}</ref> | area_code = | footnotes = }}
'''Tenshitsukinuke''' (天使突抜) is a town name located in [[:en:Shimogyō-ku, Kyoto|Shimogyō Ward]], [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto City]], [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]]. It spans both sides of the {{ill|Higashinakasuji Street|ja|東中筋通|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashinakasuji Street|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 小川通|1=Ogawa Street|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11460614}}, running north-south between {{ill|Matsubara Street (Kyoto)|ja|松原通 (京都市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsubara Street (Kyoto)|1=Matsubara Street|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11529460}} and {{ill|Rokujō Street|ja|六条通|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokujō Street|1=Rokujō Street|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11392417}}. The name originates from the Tenshi Shrine (天使社), a former designation of {{ill|Gojō Tenjin Shrine (Kyoto City)|ja|五條天神社 (京都市)|1=Gojō Tenjin-gū|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gojō Tenjin Shrine (Kyoto City)|13=User:Immanuelle/Gojō Tenjin-gū|qq=}}, through which the Tenshitsukinuke Street (now Higashinakasuji Street) was constructed.
The area is bordered to the north by Tenjinmae-chō and consists of four sections arranged southward: 1-chōme (up to {{ill|Manjuji Street|ja|万寿寺通|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manjuji Street|1=Manjuji Street|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11353816}}), 2-chōme (up to [[:en:Gojō Street|Gojō Street]]), 3-chōme (up to {{ill|Yōbai Street|ja|楊梅通|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yōbai Street|1=Yōbai Street|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24887425}}), and 4-chōme (up to Rokujō Street).
In the 18th year of the Tenshō era (1590), [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Toyotomi Hideyoshi]] implemented a redesign of Kyoto, known as the {{ill|Tenshō Land Division|ja|天正の地割|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenshō Land Division|1=Tenshō Land Division|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11442707}}, constructing a new street through the precincts of Gojō Tenjin Shrine. As the shrine was also called Tenshi Shrine, this street was named Tenshitsukinuke Street. According to the 1915 ''Kyōto Bōmeishi,'' Tenshitsukinuke Street initially extended from {{ill|Takatsuji Street|ja|高辻通|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takatsuji Street|1=Takatsuji Street|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11672896}} to {{ill|Bukkoji Street|ja|仏光寺通|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bukkoji Street|1=Bukkōji Street|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11377503}}, later being extended to {{ill|Shio-kōji Street|ja|塩小路通|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shio-kōji Street|1=Shiokōji Street|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11428656}}.
During the Edo period, 1-chōme and 2-chōme belonged to Kawanishi Ku-chōgumi Tomi San-chōgumi, 3-chōme to Kawanishi Ku-chō Izumi-gumi, and 4-chōme to Kawanishi Ku-chō Nakasuji-gumi. In the Meiji era, the area underwent administrative changes, becoming part of the 13th group, the 17th district, and eventually incorporated into Shimogyō Ward in 1879.
== Notable residents and works == * {{ill|Mutsumi Tsūzaki|ja|通崎睦美|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mutsumi Tsūzaki|1=Mutsumi Tsūzaki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11639574}} - A resident of Tenshitsukinuke who authored essays like ''Tenshitsukinuke 1-Chōme: Kimono and Bicycles.'' * {{ill|Tatsuya Yoshimura|ja|吉村達也|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tatsuya Yoshimura|1=Tatsuya Yoshimura|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11413314}} - A novelist who wrote ''Kyoto Tenshitsukinuke-dōri no Koi'' (Kyoto's Love on Tenshitsukinuke Street). * {{ill|Masashi Yamada (director)|ja|山田雅史|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masashi Yamada (director)|1=Masafumi Yamada|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11470732}} - A film director who produced ''Tenshitsukinuke Rokuchōme.''
== References == {{Reflist|20em}}
== Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書|author=角川日本地名大辞典編纂委員会 |title=角川日本地名大辞典 |volume=26-1 京都府 上巻 |year=1982 |publisher=[[:en:Kadokawa Shoten|Kadokawa Shoten]] |ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|和書|author=糸井通浩 |author-link=糸井通浩 |title=京都地名語源辞典 |editor=吉田金彦|editor-link=吉田金彦|editor2=糸井通浩|editor3=綱本逸雄|year=2013 |publisher=[[Tokyo-dō Shuppan]] |isbn=978-4-490-10841-5 |ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|和書|title=京都の地名由来辞典 |editor=源城政好|editor2=下坂守|editor2-link=下坂守|year=2005 |publisher=[[Tokyo-dō Shuppan]] |isbn=4-490-10683-1 |ref=harv}}
== See also == * {{ill|Mutsumi Tsūzaki|ja|通崎睦美|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mutsumi Tsūzaki|1=Mutsumi Tsūzaki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11639574}} * {{ill|Tatsuya Yoshimura|ja|吉村達也|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tatsuya Yoshimura|1=Tatsuya Yoshimura|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11413314}} * {{ill|Masashi Yamada (director)|ja|山田雅史|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masashi Yamada (director)|1=Masafumi Yamada|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11470732}}

'''Tenshitsukinuke''' (天使突抜) is a town name located in [[:en:Shimogyō-ku, Kyoto|Shimogyō Ward]], [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto City]], [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]]. It spans both sides of the {{ill|Higashinakasuji Street|ja|東中筋通|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashinakasuji Street|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 小川通|1=Ogawa Street|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11460614}}, running north-south between {{ill|Matsubara Street (Kyoto)|ja|松原通 (京都市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsubara Street (Kyoto)|1=Matsubara Street|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11529460}} and {{ill|Rokujō Street|ja|六条通|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokujō Street|1=Rokujō Street|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11392417}}. The name originates from the Tenshi Shrine (天使社), a former designation of {{ill|Gojō Tenjin Shrine (Kyoto City)|ja|五條天神社 (京都市)|1=Gojō Tenjin-gū|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gojō Tenjin Shrine (Kyoto City)|13=User:Immanuelle/Gojō Tenjin-gū|qq=}}, through which the Tenshitsukinuke Street (now Higashinakasuji Street) was constructed.
The area is bordered to the north by Tenjinmae-chō and consists of four sections arranged southward: 1-chōme (up to {{ill|Manjuji Street|ja|万寿寺通|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manjuji Street|1=Manjuji Street|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11353816}}), 2-chōme (up to [[:en:Gojō Street|Gojō Street]]), 3-chōme (up to {{ill|Yōbai Street|ja|楊梅通|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yōbai Street|1=Yōbai Street|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24887425}}), and 4-chōme (up to Rokujō Street).
In the 18th year of the Tenshō era (1590), [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Toyotomi Hideyoshi]] implemented a redesign of Kyoto, known as the {{ill|Tenshō Land Division|ja|天正の地割|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenshō Land Division|1=Tenshō Land Division|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11442707}}, constructing a new street through the precincts of Gojō Tenjin Shrine. As the shrine was also called Tenshi Shrine, this street was named Tenshitsukinuke Street. According to the 1915 ''Kyōto Bōmeishi,'' Tenshitsukinuke Street initially extended from {{ill|Takatsuji Street|ja|高辻通|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takatsuji Street|1=Takatsuji Street|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11672896}} to {{ill|Bukkoji Street|ja|仏光寺通|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bukkoji Street|1=Bukkōji Street|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11377503}}, later being extended to {{ill|Shio-kōji Street|ja|塩小路通|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shio-kōji Street|1=Shiokōji Street|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11428656}}.
During the Edo period, 1-chōme and 2-chōme belonged to Kawanishi Ku-chōgumi Tomi San-chōgumi, 3-chōme to Kawanishi Ku-chō Izumi-gumi, and 4-chōme to Kawanishi Ku-chō Nakasuji-gumi. In the Meiji era, the area underwent administrative changes, becoming part of the 13th group, the 17th district, and eventually incorporated into Shimogyō Ward in 1879.
== Notable residents and works == * {{ill|Mutsumi Tsūzaki|ja|通崎睦美|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mutsumi Tsūzaki|1=Mutsumi Tsūzaki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11639574}} - A resident of Tenshitsukinuke who authored essays like ''Tenshitsukinuke 1-Chōme: Kimono and Bicycles.'' * {{ill|Tatsuya Yoshimura|ja|吉村達也|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tatsuya Yoshimura|1=Tatsuya Yoshimura|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11413314}} - A novelist who wrote ''Kyoto Tenshitsukinuke-dōri no Koi'' (Kyoto's Love on Tenshitsukinuke Street). * {{ill|Masashi Yamada (director)|ja|山田雅史|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masashi Yamada (director)|1=Masafumi Yamada|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11470732}} - A film director who produced ''Tenshitsukinuke Rokuchōme.''

footnotes = }}
'''Tenshitsukinuke''' (天使突抜) is a town name located in [[:en:Shimogyō-ku, Kyoto|Shimogyō Ward]], [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto City]], [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]]. It spans both sides of the {{ill|Higashinakasuji Street|ja|東中筋通|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashinakasuji Street|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 小川通|1=Ogawa Street|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11460614}}, running north-south between {{ill|Matsubara Street (Kyoto)|ja|松原通 (京都市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsubara Street (Kyoto)|1=Matsubara Street|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11529460}} and {{ill|Rokujō Street|ja|六条通|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokujō Street|1=Rokujō Street|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11392417}}. The name originates from the Tenshi Shrine (天使社), a former designation of {{ill|Gojō Tenjin Shrine (Kyoto City)|ja|五條天神社 (京都市)|1=Gojō Tenjin-gū|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gojō Tenjin Shrine (Kyoto City)|13=User:Immanuelle/Gojō Tenjin-gū|qq=}}, through which the Tenshitsukinuke Street (now Higashinakasuji Street) was constructed.
The area is bordered to the north by Tenjinmae-chō and consists of four sections arranged southward: 1-chōme (up to {{ill|Manjuji Street|ja|万寿寺通|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manjuji Street|1=Manjuji Street|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11353816}}), 2-chōme (up to [[:en:Gojō Street|Gojō Street]]), 3-chōme (up to {{ill|Yōbai Street|ja|楊梅通|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yōbai Street|1=Yōbai Street|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24887425}}), and 4-chōme (up to Rokujō Street).
In the 18th year of the Tenshō era (1590), [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Toyotomi Hideyoshi]] implemented a redesign of Kyoto, known as the {{ill|Tenshō Land Division|ja|天正の地割|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenshō Land Division|1=Tenshō Land Division|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11442707}}, constructing a new street through the precincts of Gojō Tenjin Shrine. As the shrine was also called Tenshi Shrine, this street was named Tenshitsukinuke Street. According to the 1915 ''Kyōto Bōmeishi,'' Tenshitsukinuke Street initially extended from {{ill|Takatsuji Street|ja|高辻通|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takatsuji Street|1=Takatsuji Street|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11672896}} to {{ill|Bukkoji Street|ja|仏光寺通|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bukkoji Street|1=Bukkōji Street|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11377503}}, later being extended to {{ill|Shio-kōji Street|ja|塩小路通|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shio-kōji Street|1=Shiokōji Street|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11428656}}.
During the Edo period, 1-chōme and 2-chōme belonged to Kawanishi Ku-chōgumi Tomi San-chōgumi, 3-chōme to Kawanishi Ku-chō Izumi-gumi, and 4-chōme to Kawanishi Ku-chō Nakasuji-gumi. In the Meiji era, the area underwent administrative changes, becoming part of the 13th group, the 17th district, and eventually incorporated into Shimogyō Ward in 1879.
== Notable residents and works == * {{ill|Mutsumi Tsūzaki|ja|通崎睦美|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mutsumi Tsūzaki|1=Mutsumi Tsūzaki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11639574}} - A resident of Tenshitsukinuke who authored essays like ''Tenshitsukinuke 1-Chōme: Kimono and Bicycles.'' * {{ill|Tatsuya Yoshimura|ja|吉村達也|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tatsuya Yoshimura|1=Tatsuya Yoshimura|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11413314}} - A novelist who wrote ''Kyoto Tenshitsukinuke-dōri no Koi'' (Kyoto's Love on Tenshitsukinuke Street). * {{ill|Masashi Yamada (director)|ja|山田雅史|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masashi Yamada (director)|1=Masafumi Yamada|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11470732}} - A film director who produced ''Tenshitsukinuke Rokuchōme.''

In the Meiji era, the area underwent administrative changes, becoming part of the 13th group, the 17th district, and eventually incorporated into Shimogyō Ward in 1879.
== Notable residents and works == * {{ill|Mutsumi Tsūzaki|ja|通崎睦美|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mutsumi Tsūzaki|1=Mutsumi Tsūzaki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11639574}} - A resident of Tenshitsukinuke who authored essays like ''Tenshitsukinuke 1-Chōme: Kimono and Bicycles.'' * {{ill|Tatsuya Yoshimura|ja|吉村達也|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tatsuya Yoshimura|1=Tatsuya Yoshimura|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11413314}} - A novelist who wrote ''Kyoto Tenshitsukinuke-dōri no Koi'' (Kyoto's Love on Tenshitsukinuke Street). * {{ill|Masashi Yamada (director)|ja|山田雅史|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masashi Yamada (director)|1=Masafumi Yamada|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11470732}} - A film director who produced ''Tenshitsukinuke Rokuchōme.''

footnotes = }}
'''Tenshitsukinuke''' (天使突抜) is a town name located in [[:en:Shimogyō-ku, Kyoto|Shimogyō Ward]], [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto City]], [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]]. It spans both sides of the {{ill|Higashinakasuji Street|ja|東中筋通|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashinakasuji Street|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 小川通|1=Ogawa Street|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11460614}}, running north-south between {{ill|Matsubara Street (Kyoto)|ja|松原通 (京都市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsubara Street (Kyoto)|1=Matsubara Street|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11529460}} and {{ill|Rokujō Street|ja|六条通|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokujō Street|1=Rokujō Street|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11392417}}. The name originates from the Tenshi Shrine (天使社), a former designation of {{ill|Gojō Tenjin Shrine (Kyoto City)|ja|五條天神社 (京都市)|1=Gojō Tenjin-gū|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gojō Tenjin Shrine (Kyoto City)|13=User:Immanuelle/Gojō Tenjin-gū|qq=}}, through which the Tenshitsukinuke Street (now Higashinakasuji Street) was constructed.
The area is bordered to the north by Tenjinmae-chō and consists of four sections arranged southward: 1-chōme (up to {{ill|Manjuji Street|ja|万寿寺通|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manjuji Street|1=Manjuji Street|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11353816}}), 2-chōme (up to [[:en:Gojō Street|Gojō Street]]), 3-chōme (up to {{ill|Yōbai Street|ja|楊梅通|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yōbai Street|1=Yōbai Street|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24887425}}), and 4-chōme (up to Rokujō Street).
In the 18th year of the Tenshō era (1590), [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Toyotomi Hideyoshi]] implemented a redesign of Kyoto, known as the {{ill|Tenshō Land Division|ja|天正の地割|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenshō Land Division|1=Tenshō Land Division|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11442707}}, constructing a new street through the precincts of Gojō Tenjin Shrine. As the shrine was also called Tenshi Shrine, this street was named Tenshitsukinuke Street. According to the 1915 ''Kyōto Bōmeishi,'' Tenshitsukinuke Street initially extended from {{ill|Takatsuji Street|ja|高辻通|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takatsuji Street|1=Takatsuji Street|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11672896}} to {{ill|Bukkoji Street|ja|仏光寺通|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bukkoji Street|1=Bukkōji Street|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11377503}}, later being extended to {{ill|Shio-kōji Street|ja|塩小路通|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shio-kōji Street|1=Shiokōji Street|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11428656}}.
During the Edo period, 1-chōme and 2-chōme belonged to Kawanishi Ku-chōgumi Tomi San-chōgumi, 3-chōme to Kawanishi Ku-chō Izumi-gumi, and 4-chōme to Kawanishi Ku-chō Nakasuji-gumi. In the Meiji era, the area underwent administrative changes, becoming part of the 13th group, the 17th district, and eventually incorporated into Shimogyō Ward in 1879.
== Notable residents and works == * {{ill|Mutsumi Tsūzaki|ja|通崎睦美|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mutsumi Tsūzaki|1=Mutsumi Tsūzaki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11639574}} - A resident of Tenshitsukinuke who authored essays like ''Tenshitsukinuke 1-Chōme: Kimono and Bicycles.'' * {{ill|Tatsuya Yoshimura|ja|吉村達也|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tatsuya Yoshimura|1=Tatsuya Yoshimura|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11413314}} - A novelist who wrote ''Kyoto Tenshitsukinuke-dōri no Koi'' (Kyoto's Love on Tenshitsukinuke Street). * {{ill|Masashi Yamada (director)|ja|山田雅史|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masashi Yamada (director)|1=Masafumi Yamada|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11470732}} - A film director who produced ''Tenshitsukinuke Rokuchōme.''

footnotes = }}
'''Tenshitsukinuke''' (天使突抜) is a town name located in [[:en:Shimogyō-ku, Kyoto|Shimogyō Ward]], [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto City]], [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]]. It spans both sides of the {{ill|Higashinakasuji Street|ja|東中筋通|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashinakasuji Street|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 小川通|1=Ogawa Street|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11460614}}, running north-south between {{ill|Matsubara Street (Kyoto)|ja|松原通 (京都市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsubara Street (Kyoto)|1=Matsubara Street|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11529460}} and {{ill|Rokujō Street|ja|六条通|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokujō Street|1=Rokujō Street|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11392417}}. The name originates from the Tenshi Shrine (天使社), a former designation of {{ill|Gojō Tenjin Shrine (Kyoto City)|ja|五條天神社 (京都市)|1=Gojō Tenjin-gū|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gojō Tenjin Shrine (Kyoto City)|13=User:Immanuelle/Gojō Tenjin-gū|qq=}}, through which the Tenshitsukinuke Street (now Higashinakasuji Street) was constructed.
The area is bordered to the north by Tenjinmae-chō and consists of four sections arranged southward: 1-chōme (up to {{ill|Manjuji Street|ja|万寿寺通|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manjuji Street|1=Manjuji Street|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11353816}}), 2-chōme (up to [[:en:Gojō Street|Gojō Street]]), 3-chōme (up to {{ill|Yōbai Street|ja|楊梅通|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yōbai Street|1=Yōbai Street|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24887425}}), and 4-chōme (up to Rokujō Street).
In the 18th year of the Tenshō era (1590), [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Toyotomi Hideyoshi]] implemented a redesign of Kyoto, known as the {{ill|Tenshō Land Division|ja|天正の地割|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenshō Land Division|1=Tenshō Land Division|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11442707}}, constructing a new street through the precincts of Gojō Tenjin Shrine. As the shrine was also called Tenshi Shrine, this street was named Tenshitsukinuke Street. According to the 1915 ''Kyōto Bōmeishi,'' Tenshitsukinuke Street initially extended from {{ill|Takatsuji Street|ja|高辻通|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takatsuji Street|1=Takatsuji Street|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11672896}} to {{ill|Bukkoji Street|ja|仏光寺通|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bukkoji Street|1=Bukkōji Street|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11377503}}, later being extended to {{ill|Shio-kōji Street|ja|塩小路通|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shio-kōji Street|1=Shiokōji Street|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11428656}}.
During the Edo period, 1-chōme and 2-chōme belonged to Kawanishi Ku-chōgumi Tomi San-chōgumi, 3-chōme to Kawanishi Ku-chō Izumi-gumi, and 4-chōme to Kawanishi Ku-chō Nakasuji-gumi. In the Meiji era, the area underwent administrative changes, becoming part of the 13th group, the 17th district, and eventually incorporated into Shimogyō Ward in 1879.
== Notable residents and works == * {{ill|Mutsumi Tsūzaki|ja|通崎睦美|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mutsumi Tsūzaki|1=Mutsumi Tsūzaki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11639574}} - A resident of Tenshitsukinuke who authored essays like ''Tenshitsukinuke 1-Chōme: Kimono and Bicycles.'' * {{ill|Tatsuya Yoshimura|ja|吉村達也|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tatsuya Yoshimura|1=Tatsuya Yoshimura|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11413314}} - A novelist who wrote ''Kyoto Tenshitsukinuke-dōri no Koi'' (Kyoto's Love on Tenshitsukinuke Street). * {{ill|Masashi Yamada (director)|ja|山田雅史|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masashi Yamada (director)|1=Masafumi Yamada|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11470732}} - A film director who produced ''Tenshitsukinuke Rokuchōme.''
== References == {{Reflist|20em}}
== Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書|author=角川日本地名大辞典編纂委員会 |title=角川日本地名大辞典 |volume=26-1 京都府 上巻 |year=1982 |publisher=[[:en:Kadokawa Shoten|Kadokawa Shoten]] |ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|和書|author=糸井通浩 |author-link=糸井通浩 |title=京都地名語源辞典 |editor=吉田金彦|editor-link=吉田金彦|editor2=糸井通浩|editor3=綱本逸雄|year=2013 |publisher=[[Tokyo-dō Shuppan]] |isbn=978-4-490-10841-5 |ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|和書|title=京都の地名由来辞典 |editor=源城政好|editor2=下坂守|editor2-link=下坂守|year=2005 |publisher=[[Tokyo-dō Shuppan]] |isbn=4-490-10683-1 |ref=harv}}

In the Meiji era, the area underwent administrative changes, becoming part of the 13th group, the 17th district, and eventually incorporated into Shimogyō Ward in 1879.

footnotes = }}
'''Tenshitsukinuke''' (天使突抜) is a town name located in [[:en:Shimogyō-ku, Kyoto|Shimogyō Ward]], [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto City]], [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]]. It spans both sides of the {{ill|Higashinakasuji Street|ja|東中筋通|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashinakasuji Street|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 小川通|1=Ogawa Street|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11460614}}, running north-south between {{ill|Matsubara Street (Kyoto)|ja|松原通 (京都市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsubara Street (Kyoto)|1=Matsubara Street|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11529460}} and {{ill|Rokujō Street|ja|六条通|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokujō Street|1=Rokujō Street|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11392417}}. The name originates from the Tenshi Shrine (天使社), a former designation of {{ill|Gojō Tenjin Shrine (Kyoto City)|ja|五條天神社 (京都市)|1=Gojō Tenjin-gū|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gojō Tenjin Shrine (Kyoto City)|13=User:Immanuelle/Gojō Tenjin-gū|qq=}}, through which the Tenshitsukinuke Street (now Higashinakasuji Street) was constructed.
The area is bordered to the north by Tenjinmae-chō and consists of four sections arranged southward: 1-chōme (up to {{ill|Manjuji Street|ja|万寿寺通|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manjuji Street|1=Manjuji Street|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11353816}}), 2-chōme (up to [[:en:Gojō Street|Gojō Street]]), 3-chōme (up to {{ill|Yōbai Street|ja|楊梅通|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yōbai Street|1=Yōbai Street|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24887425}}), and 4-chōme (up to Rokujō Street).
In the 18th year of the Tenshō era (1590), [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Toyotomi Hideyoshi]] implemented a redesign of Kyoto, known as the {{ill|Tenshō Land Division|ja|天正の地割|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenshō Land Division|1=Tenshō Land Division|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11442707}}, constructing a new street through the precincts of Gojō Tenjin Shrine. As the shrine was also called Tenshi Shrine, this street was named Tenshitsukinuke Street. According to the 1915 ''Kyōto Bōmeishi,'' Tenshitsukinuke Street initially extended from {{ill|Takatsuji Street|ja|高辻通|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takatsuji Street|1=Takatsuji Street|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11672896}} to {{ill|Bukkoji Street|ja|仏光寺通|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bukkoji Street|1=Bukkōji Street|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11377503}}, later being extended to {{ill|Shio-kōji Street|ja|塩小路通|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shio-kōji Street|1=Shiokōji Street|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11428656}}.
During the Edo period, 1-chōme and 2-chōme belonged to Kawanishi Ku-chōgumi Tomi San-chōgumi, 3-chōme to Kawanishi Ku-chō Izumi-gumi, and 4-chōme to Kawanishi Ku-chō Nakasuji-gumi. In the Meiji era, the area underwent administrative changes, becoming part of the 13th group, the 17th district, and eventually incorporated into Shimogyō Ward in 1879.
== Notable residents and works == * {{ill|Mutsumi Tsūzaki|ja|通崎睦美|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mutsumi Tsūzaki|1=Mutsumi Tsūzaki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11639574}} - A resident of Tenshitsukinuke who authored essays like ''Tenshitsukinuke 1-Chōme: Kimono and Bicycles.'' * {{ill|Tatsuya Yoshimura|ja|吉村達也|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tatsuya Yoshimura|1=Tatsuya Yoshimura|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11413314}} - A novelist who wrote ''Kyoto Tenshitsukinuke-dōri no Koi'' (Kyoto's Love on Tenshitsukinuke Street). * {{ill|Masashi Yamada (director)|ja|山田雅史|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masashi Yamada (director)|1=Masafumi Yamada|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11470732}} - A film director who produced ''Tenshitsukinuke Rokuchōme.''

Bureau|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11375707}} | footnotes = }}
'''Tenshitsukinuke''' (天使突抜) is a town name located in [[:en:Shimogyō-ku, Kyoto|Shimogyō Ward]], [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto City]], [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]]. It spans both sides of the {{ill|Higashinakasuji Street|ja|東中筋通|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashinakasuji Street|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 小川通|1=Ogawa Street|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11460614}}, running north-south between {{ill|Matsubara Street (Kyoto)|ja|松原通 (京都市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsubara Street (Kyoto)|1=Matsubara Street|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11529460}} and {{ill|Rokujō Street|ja|六条通|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokujō Street|1=Rokujō Street|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11392417}}. The name originates from the Tenshi Shrine (天使社), a former designation of {{ill|Gojō Tenjin Shrine (Kyoto City)|ja|五條天神社 (京都市)|1=Gojō Tenjin-gū|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gojō Tenjin Shrine (Kyoto City)|13=User:Immanuelle/Gojō Tenjin-gū|qq=}}, through which the Tenshitsukinuke Street (now Higashinakasuji Street) was constructed.
The area is bordered to the north by Tenjinmae-chō and consists of four sections arranged southward: 1-chōme (up to {{ill|Manjuji Street|ja|万寿寺通|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manjuji Street|1=Manjuji Street|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11353816}}), 2-chōme (up to [[:en:Gojō Street|Gojō Street]]), 3-chōme (up to {{ill|Yōbai Street|ja|楊梅通|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yōbai Street|1=Yōbai Street|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24887425}}), and 4-chōme (up to Rokujō Street).
In the 18th year of the Tenshō era (1590), [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Toyotomi Hideyoshi]] implemented a redesign of Kyoto, known as the {{ill|Tenshō Land Division|ja|天正の地割|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenshō Land Division|1=Tenshō Land Division|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11442707}}, constructing a new street through the precincts of Gojō Tenjin Shrine. As the shrine was also called Tenshi Shrine, this street was named Tenshitsukinuke Street. According to the 1915 ''Kyōto Bōmeishi,'' Tenshitsukinuke Street initially extended from {{ill|Takatsuji Street|ja|高辻通|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takatsuji Street|1=Takatsuji Street|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11672896}} to {{ill|Bukkoji Street|ja|仏光寺通|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bukkoji Street|1=Bukkōji Street|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11377503}}, later being extended to {{ill|Shio-kōji Street|ja|塩小路通|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shio-kōji Street|1=Shiokōji Street|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11428656}}.
During the Edo period, 1-chōme and 2-chōme belonged to Kawanishi Ku-chōgumi Tomi San-chōgumi, 3-chōme to Kawanishi Ku-chō Izumi-gumi, and 4-chōme to Kawanishi Ku-chō Nakasuji-gumi. In the Meiji era, the area underwent administrative changes, becoming part of the 13th group, the 17th district, and eventually incorporated into Shimogyō Ward in 1879.
== Notable residents and works == * {{ill|Mutsumi Tsūzaki|ja|通崎睦美|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mutsumi Tsūzaki|1=Mutsumi Tsūzaki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11639574}} - A resident of Tenshitsukinuke who authored essays like ''Tenshitsukinuke 1-Chōme: Kimono and Bicycles.'' * {{ill|Tatsuya Yoshimura|ja|吉村達也|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tatsuya Yoshimura|1=Tatsuya Yoshimura|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11413314}} - A novelist who wrote ''Kyoto Tenshitsukinuke-dōri no Koi'' (Kyoto's Love on Tenshitsukinuke Street). * {{ill|Masashi Yamada (director)|ja|山田雅史|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masashi Yamada (director)|1=Masafumi Yamada|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11470732}} - A film director who produced ''Tenshitsukinuke Rokuchōme.''
== References == {{Reflist|20em}}
== Bibliography == * {{Cite book|和書|author=角川日本地名大辞典編纂委員会 |title=角川日本地名大辞典 |volume=26-1 京都府 上巻 |year=1982 |publisher=[[:en:Kadokawa Shoten|Kadokawa Shoten]] |ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|和書|author=糸井通浩 |author-link=糸井通浩 |title=京都地名語源辞典 |editor=吉田金彦|editor-link=吉田金彦|editor2=糸井通浩|editor3=綱本逸雄|year=2013 |publisher=[[Tokyo-dō Shuppan]] |isbn=978-4-490-10841-5 |ref=harv}} * {{Cite book|和書|title=京都の地名由来辞典 |editor=源城政好|editor2=下坂守|editor2-link=下坂守|year=2005 |publisher=[[Tokyo-dō Shuppan]] |isbn=4-490-10683-1 |ref=harv}}

In the Meiji era, the area underwent administrative changes, becoming part of the 13th group, the 17th district, and eventually incorporated into Shimogyō Ward in 1879.

'''Tenshitsukinuke''' (天使突抜) is a town name located in [[:en:Shimogyō-ku, Kyoto|Shimogyō Ward]], [[:en:Kyoto|Kyoto City]], [[:en:Kyoto Prefecture|Kyoto Prefecture]]. It spans both sides of the {{ill|Higashinakasuji Street|ja|東中筋通|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashinakasuji Street|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 小川通|1=Ogawa Street|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11460614}}, running north-south between {{ill|Matsubara Street (Kyoto)|ja|松原通 (京都市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsubara Street (Kyoto)|1=Matsubara Street|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11529460}} and {{ill|Rokujō Street|ja|六条通|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokujō Street|1=Rokujō Street|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11392417}}. The name originates from the Tenshi Shrine (天使社), a former designation of {{ill|Gojō Tenjin Shrine (Kyoto City)|ja|五條天神社 (京都市)|1=Gojō Tenjin-gū|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gojō Tenjin Shrine (Kyoto City)|13=User:Immanuelle/Gojō Tenjin-gū|qq=}}, through which the Tenshitsukinuke Street (now Higashinakasuji Street) was constructed.
The area is bordered to the north by Tenjinmae-chō and consists of four sections arranged southward: 1-chōme (up to {{ill|Manjuji Street|ja|万寿寺通|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manjuji Street|1=Manjuji Street|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11353816}}), 2-chōme (up to [[:en:Gojō Street|Gojō Street]]), 3-chōme (up to {{ill|Yōbai Street|ja|楊梅通|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yōbai Street|1=Yōbai Street|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24887425}}), and 4-chōme (up to Rokujō Street).
In the 18th year of the Tenshō era (1590), [[:en:Toyotomi Hideyoshi|Toyotomi Hideyoshi]] implemented a redesign of Kyoto, known as the {{ill|Tenshō Land Division|ja|天正の地割|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenshō Land Division|1=Tenshō Land Division|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11442707}}, constructing a new street through the precincts of Gojō Tenjin Shrine. As the shrine was also called Tenshi Shrine, this street was named Tenshitsukinuke Street. According to the 1915 ''Kyōto Bōmeishi,'' Tenshitsukinuke Street initially extended from {{ill|Takatsuji Street|ja|高辻通|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takatsuji Street|1=Takatsuji Street|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11672896}} to {{ill|Bukkoji Street|ja|仏光寺通|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bukkoji Street|1=Bukkōji Street|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11377503}}, later being extended to {{ill|Shio-kōji Street|ja|塩小路通|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shio-kōji Street|1=Shiokōji Street|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11428656}}.
During the Edo period, 1-chōme and 2-chōme belonged to Kawanishi Ku-chōgumi Tomi San-chōgumi, 3-chōme to Kawanishi Ku-chō Izumi-gumi, and 4-chōme to Kawanishi Ku-chō Nakasuji-gumi. In the Meiji era, the area underwent administrative changes, becoming part of the 13th group, the 17th district, and eventually incorporated into Shimogyō Ward in 1879.
== Notable residents and works == * {{ill|Mutsumi Tsūzaki|ja|通崎睦美|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mutsumi Tsūzaki|1=Mutsumi Tsūzaki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11639574}} - A resident of Tenshitsukinuke who authored essays like ''Tenshitsukinuke 1-Chōme: Kimono and Bicycles.'' * {{ill|Tatsuya Yoshimura|ja|吉村達也|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tatsuya Yoshimura|1=Tatsuya Yoshimura|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11413314}} - A novelist who wrote ''Kyoto Tenshitsukinuke-dōri no Koi'' (Kyoto's Love on Tenshitsukinuke Street). * {{ill|Masashi Yamada (director)|ja|山田雅史|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masashi Yamada (director)|1=Masafumi Yamada|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11470732}} - A film director who produced ''Tenshitsukinuke Rokuchōme.''

'''Tenshitsukinuke''' (天使突抜) is a town name located in {{ill|Shimogyō-ku, Kyoto|en|Shimogyō-ku, Kyoto|WD=Q1077022|lt=Shimogyō Ward}}, {{ill|Kyoto|en|Kyoto|WD=Q34600|lt=Kyoto City}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|en|Kyoto Prefecture|WD=Q120730|lt=Kyoto Prefecture}}. It spans both sides of the {{ill|Higashinakasuji Street|ja|東中筋通|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashinakasuji Street|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 小川通|1=Ogawa Street|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11460614}}, running north-south between {{ill|Matsubara Street (Kyoto)|ja|松原通 (京都市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsubara Street (Kyoto)|1=Matsubara Street|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11529460}} and {{ill|Rokujō Street|ja|六条通|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokujō Street|1=Rokujō Street|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11392417}}. The name originates from the Tenshi Shrine (天使社), a former designation of {{ill|Gojō Tenjin Shrine (Kyoto City)|ja|五條天神社 (京都市)|1=Gojō Tenjin-gū|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gojō Tenjin Shrine (Kyoto City)|13=User:Immanuelle/Gojō Tenjin-gū|qq=}}, through which the Tenshitsukinuke Street (now Higashinakasuji Street) was constructed.

The area is bordered to the north by Tenjinmae-chō and consists of four sections arranged southward: 1-chōme (up to {{ill|Manjuji Street|ja|万寿寺通|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manjuji Street|1=Manjuji Street|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11353816}}), 2-chōme (up to {{ill|Gojō Street|en|Gojō Street|WD=Q11372492|lt=Gojō Street}}), 3-chōme (up to {{ill|Yōbai Street|ja|楊梅通|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yōbai Street|1=Yōbai Street|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24887425}}), and 4-chōme (up to Rokujō Street).

In the 18th year of the Tenshō era (1590), {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|en|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|WD=Q187550|lt=Toyotomi Hideyoshi}} implemented a redesign of Kyoto, known as the {{ill|Tenshō Land Division|ja|天正の地割|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenshō Land Division|1=Tenshō Land Division|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11442707}}, constructing a new street through the precincts of Gojō Tenjin Shrine. As the shrine was also called Tenshi Shrine, this street was named Tenshitsukinuke Street. According to the 1915 ''Kyōto Bōmeishi,'' Tenshitsukinuke Street initially extended from {{ill|Takatsuji Street|ja|高辻通|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takatsuji Street|1=Takatsuji Street|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11672896}} to {{ill|Bukkoji Street|ja|仏光寺通|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bukkoji Street|1=Bukkōji Street|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11377503}}, later being extended to {{ill|Shio-kōji Street|ja|塩小路通|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shio-kōji Street|1=Shiokōji Street|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11428656}}.

During the Edo period, 1-chōme and 2-chōme belonged to Kawanishi Ku-chōgumi Tomi San-chōgumi, 3-chōme to Kawanishi Ku-chō Izumi-gumi, and 4-chōme to Kawanishi Ku-chō Nakasuji-gumi. In the Meiji era, the area underwent administrative changes, becoming part of the 13th group, the 17th district, and eventually incorporated into Shimogyō Ward in 1879.

== Notable residents and works ==
* {{ill|Mutsumi Tsūzaki|ja|通崎睦美|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mutsumi Tsūzaki|1=Mutsumi Tsūzaki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11639574}} - A resident of Tenshitsukinuke who authored essays like ''Tenshitsukinuke 1-Chōme: Kimono and Bicycles.''
* {{ill|Tatsuya Yoshimura|ja|吉村達也|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tatsuya Yoshimura|1=Tatsuya Yoshimura|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11413314}} - A novelist who wrote ''Kyoto Tenshitsukinuke-dōri no Koi'' (Kyoto's Love on Tenshitsukinuke Street).
* {{ill|Masashi Yamada (director)|ja|山田雅史|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masashi Yamada (director)|1=Masafumi Yamada|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11470732}} - A film director who produced ''Tenshitsukinuke Rokuchōme.''

'''Tenshitsukinuke''' (天使突抜) is a town name located in {{ill|Shimogyō-ku, Kyoto|en|Shimogyō-ku, Kyoto|WD=Q1077022|lt=Shimogyō Ward}}, {{ill|Kyoto|en|Kyoto|WD=Q34600|lt=Kyoto City}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|en|Kyoto Prefecture|WD=Q120730|lt=Kyoto Prefecture}}. It spans both sides of the {{ill|Higashinakasuji Street|ja|東中筋通|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashinakasuji Street|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 小川通|1=Ogawa Street|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11460614}}, running north-south between {{ill|Matsubara Street (Kyoto)|ja|松原通 (京都市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsubara Street (Kyoto)|1=Matsubara Street|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11529460}} and {{ill|Rokujō Street|ja|六条通|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokujō Street|1=Rokujō Street|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11392417}}. The name originates from the Tenshi Shrine (天使社), a former designation of {{ill|Gojō Tenjin Shrine (Kyoto City)|ja|五條天神社 (京都市)|1=Gojō Tenjin-gū|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gojō Tenjin Shrine (Kyoto City)|13=User:Immanuelle/Gojō Tenjin-gū|qq=}}, through which the Tenshitsukinuke Street (now Higashinakasuji Street) was constructed.

The area is bordered to the north by Tenjinmae-chō and consists of four sections arranged southward: 1-chōme (up to {{ill|Manjuji Street|ja|万寿寺通|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manjuji Street|1=Manjuji Street|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11353816}}), 2-chōme (up to {{ill|Gojō Street|en|Gojō Street|WD=Q11372492|lt=Gojō Street}}), 3-chōme (up to {{ill|Yōbai Street|ja|楊梅通|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yōbai Street|1=Yōbai Street|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24887425}}), and 4-chōme (up to Rokujō Street).

In the 18th year of the Tenshō era (1590), {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|en|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|WD=Q187550|lt=Toyotomi Hideyoshi}} implemented a redesign of Kyoto, known as the {{ill|Tenshō Land Division|ja|天正の地割|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenshō Land Division|1=Tenshō Land Division|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11442707}}, constructing a new street through the precincts of Gojō Tenjin Shrine. As the shrine was also called Tenshi Shrine, this street was named Tenshitsukinuke Street. According to the 1915 ''Kyōto Bōmeishi,'' Tenshitsukinuke Street initially extended from {{ill|Takatsuji Street|ja|高辻通|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takatsuji Street|1=Takatsuji Street|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11672896}} to {{ill|Bukkoji Street|ja|仏光寺通|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bukkoji Street|1=Bukkōji Street|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11377503}}, later being extended to {{ill|Shio-kōji Street|ja|塩小路通|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shio-kōji Street|1=Shiokōji Street|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11428656}}.

During the Edo period, 1-chōme and 2-chōme belonged to Kawanishi Ku-chōgumi Tomi San-chōgumi, 3-chōme to Kawanishi Ku-chō Izumi-gumi, and 4-chōme to Kawanishi Ku-chō Nakasuji-gumi. In the Meiji era, the area underwent administrative changes, becoming part of the 13th group, the 17th district, and eventually incorporated into Shimogyō Ward in 1879.

== Notable residents and works ==
* {{ill|Mutsumi Tsūzaki|ja|通崎睦美|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mutsumi Tsūzaki|1=Mutsumi Tsūzaki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11639574}} - A resident of Tenshitsukinuke who authored essays like ''Tenshitsukinuke 1-Chōme: Kimono and Bicycles.''
* {{ill|Tatsuya Yoshimura|ja|吉村達也|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tatsuya Yoshimura|1=Tatsuya Yoshimura|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11413314}} - A novelist who wrote ''Kyoto Tenshitsukinuke-dōri no Koi'' (Kyoto's Love on Tenshitsukinuke Street).
* {{ill|Masashi Yamada (director)|ja|山田雅史|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masashi Yamada (director)|1=Masafumi Yamada|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11470732}} - A film director who produced ''Tenshitsukinuke Rokuchōme.''

'''Tenshitsukinuke''' (天使突抜) is a town name located in {{ill|Shimogyō-ku, Kyoto|en|Shimogyō-ku, Kyoto|WD=Q1077022|lt=Shimogyō Ward|1=Shimogyō-ku, Kyoto}}, {{ill|Kyoto|en|Kyoto|WD=Q34600|lt=Kyoto City|1=Kyoto}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|en|Kyoto Prefecture|WD=Q120730|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}. It spans both sides of the {{ill|Higashinakasuji Street|ja|東中筋通|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashinakasuji Street|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 小川通|1=Ogawa Street|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11460614}}, running north-south between {{ill|Matsubara Street (Kyoto)|ja|松原通 (京都市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsubara Street (Kyoto)|1=Matsubara Street|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11529460}} and {{ill|Rokujō Street|ja|六条通|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokujō Street|1=Rokujō Street|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11392417}}. The name originates from the Tenshi Shrine (天使社), a former designation of {{ill|Gojō Tenjin Shrine (Kyoto City)|ja|五條天神社 (京都市)|1=Gojō Tenjin-gū|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gojō Tenjin Shrine (Kyoto City)|13=User:Immanuelle/Gojō Tenjin-gū|qq=}}, through which the Tenshitsukinuke Street (now Higashinakasuji Street) was constructed.

The area is bordered to the north by Tenjinmae-chō and consists of four sections arranged southward: 1-chōme (up to {{ill|Manjuji Street|ja|万寿寺通|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manjuji Street|1=Manjuji Street|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11353816}}), 2-chōme (up to {{ill|Gojō Street|en|Gojō Street|WD=Q11372492|lt=Gojō Street|1=Gojō Street}}), 3-chōme (up to {{ill|Yōbai Street|ja|楊梅通|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yōbai Street|1=Yōbai Street|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24887425}}), and 4-chōme (up to Rokujō Street).

In the 18th year of the Tenshō era (1590), {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|en|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|WD=Q187550|lt=Toyotomi Hideyoshi|1=Toyotomi Hideyoshi}} implemented a redesign of Kyoto, known as the {{ill|Tenshō Land Division|ja|天正の地割|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenshō Land Division|1=Q11442707|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11442707}}, constructing a new street through the precincts of Gojō Tenjin Shrine. As the shrine was also called Tenshi Shrine, this street was named Tenshitsukinuke Street. According to the 1915 ''Kyōto Bōmeishi,'' Tenshitsukinuke Street initially extended from {{ill|Takatsuji Street|ja|高辻通|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takatsuji Street|1=Takatsuji Street|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11672896}} to {{ill|Bukkoji Street|ja|仏光寺通|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bukkoji Street|1=Bukkōji Street|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11377503}}, later being extended to {{ill|Shio-kōji Street|ja|塩小路通|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shio-kōji Street|1=Shiokōji Street|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11428656}}.

During the Edo period, 1-chōme and 2-chōme belonged to Kawanishi Ku-chōgumi Tomi San-chōgumi, 3-chōme to Kawanishi Ku-chō Izumi-gumi, and 4-chōme to Kawanishi Ku-chō Nakasuji-gumi. In the Meiji era, the area underwent administrative changes, becoming part of the 13th group, the 17th district, and eventually incorporated into Shimogyō Ward in 1879.

== Notable residents and works ==

* {{ill|Tatsuya Yoshimura|ja|吉村達也|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tatsuya Yoshimura|1=Tatsuya Yoshimura|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11413314}} - A novelist who wrote ''Kyoto Tenshitsukinuke-dōri no Koi'' (Kyoto's Love on Tenshitsukinuke Street).
* {{ill|Masashi Yamada (director)|ja|山田雅史|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masashi Yamada (director)|1=Masafumi Yamada|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11470732}} - A film director who produced ''Tenshitsukinuke Rokuchōme.''

'''Tenshitsukinuke''' (天使突抜) is a town name located in {{ill|Shimogyō-ku, Kyoto|en|Shimogyō-ku, Kyoto|WD=Q1077022|lt=Shimogyō Ward|1=Shimogyō-ku, Kyoto}}, {{ill|Kyoto|en|Kyoto|WD=Q34600|lt=Kyoto City|1=Kyoto}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|en|Kyoto Prefecture|WD=Q120730|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}. The name originates from the Tenshi Shrine (天使社), a former designation of {{ill|Gojō Tenjin Shrine (Kyoto City)|ja|五條天神社 (京都市)|1=Gojō Tenjin-gū|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gojō Tenjin Shrine (Kyoto City)|13=User:Immanuelle/Gojō Tenjin-gū|qq=}}, through which the Tenshitsukinuke Street (now Higashinakasuji Street) was constructed.

In the 18th year of the Tenshō era (1590), {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|en|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|WD=Q187550|lt=Toyotomi Hideyoshi|1=Toyotomi Hideyoshi}} implemented a redesign of Kyoto, known as the {{ill|Tenshō Land Division|ja|天正の地割|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenshō Land Division|1=Q11442707|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11442707}}, constructing a new street through the precincts of Gojō Tenjin Shrine. According to the 1915 ''Kyōto Bōmeishi,'' Tenshitsukinuke Street initially extended from {{ill|Takatsuji Street|ja|高辻通|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takatsuji Street|1=Takatsuji Street|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11672896}} to {{ill|Bukkoji Street|ja|仏光寺通|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bukkoji Street|1=Bukkōji Street|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11377503}}, later being extended to {{ill|Shio-kōji Street|ja|塩小路通|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shio-kōji Street|1=Shiokōji Street|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11428656}}.

During the Edo period, 1-chōme and 2-chōme belonged to Kawanishi Ku-chōgumi Tomi San-chōgumi, 3-chōme to Kawanishi Ku-chō Izumi-gumi, and 4-chōme to Kawanishi Ku-chō Nakasuji-gumi. '''Tenshitsukinuke''' (天使突抜) is a town name located in {{ill|Shimogyō-ku, Kyoto|en|Shimogyō-ku, Kyoto|WD=Q1077022|lt=Shimogyō Ward|1=Shimogyō-ku, Kyoto}}, {{ill|Kyoto|en|Kyoto|WD=Q34600|lt=Kyoto City|1=Kyoto}}, {{ill|Kyoto Prefecture|en|Kyoto Prefecture|WD=Q120730|lt=Kyoto Prefecture|1=Kyoto Prefecture}}. It spans both sides of the {{ill|Higashinakasuji Street|ja|東中筋通|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Higashinakasuji Street|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 小川通|1=Ogawa Street|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11460614}}, running north-south between {{ill|Matsubara Street (Kyoto)|ja|松原通 (京都市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Matsubara Street (Kyoto)|1=Matsubara Street|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11529460}} and {{ill|Rokujō Street|ja|六条通|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Rokujō Street|1=Rokujō Street|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11392417}}. The name originates from the Tenshi Shrine (天使社), a former designation of {{ill|Gojō Tenjin Shrine (Kyoto City)|ja|五條天神社 (京都市)|1=Gojō Tenjin-gū|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Gojō Tenjin Shrine (Kyoto City)|13=User:Immanuelle/Gojō Tenjin-gū|qq=}}, through which the Tenshitsukinuke Street (now Higashinakasuji Street) was constructed.

The area is bordered to the north by Tenjinmae-chō and consists of four sections arranged southward: 1-chōme (up to {{ill|Manjuji Street|ja|万寿寺通|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Manjuji Street|1=Manjuji Street|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11353816}}), 2-chōme (up to {{ill|Gojō Street|en|Gojō Street|WD=Q11372492|lt=Gojō Street|1=Gojō Street}}), 3-chōme (up to {{ill|Yōbai Street|ja|楊梅通|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Yōbai Street|1=Yōbai Street|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q24887425}}), and 4-chōme (up to Rokujō Street).

In the 18th year of the Tenshō era (1590), {{ill|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|en|Toyotomi Hideyoshi|WD=Q187550|lt=Toyotomi Hideyoshi|1=Toyotomi Hideyoshi}} implemented a redesign of Kyoto, known as the {{ill|Tenshō Land Division|ja|天正の地割|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tenshō Land Division|1=Q11442707|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11442707}}, constructing a new street through the precincts of Gojō Tenjin Shrine. As the shrine was also called Tenshi Shrine, this street was named Tenshitsukinuke Street. According to the 1915 ''Kyōto Bōmeishi,'' Tenshitsukinuke Street initially extended from {{ill|Takatsuji Street|ja|高辻通|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Takatsuji Street|1=Takatsuji Street|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11672896}} to {{ill|Bukkoji Street|ja|仏光寺通|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Bukkoji Street|1=Bukkōji Street|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11377503}}, later being extended to {{ill|Shio-kōji Street|ja|塩小路通|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shio-kōji Street|1=Shiokōji Street|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11428656}}.

During the Edo period, 1-chōme and 2-chōme belonged to Kawanishi Ku-chōgumi Tomi San-chōgumi, 3-chōme to Kawanishi Ku-chō Izumi-gumi, and 4-chōme to Kawanishi Ku-chō Nakasuji-gumi. In the Meiji era, the area underwent administrative changes, becoming part of the 13th group, the 17th district, and eventually incorporated into Shimogyō Ward in 1879.

== Notable residents and works ==
* {{ill|Mutsumi Tsūzaki|ja|通崎睦美|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mutsumi Tsūzaki|1=Mutsumi Tsūzaki|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11639574}} - A resident of Tenshitsukinuke who authored essays like ''Tenshitsukinuke 1-Chōme: Kimono and Bicycles.''
* {{ill|Tatsuya Yoshimura|ja|吉村達也|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tatsuya Yoshimura|1=Tatsuya Yoshimura|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11413314}} - A novelist who wrote ''Kyoto Tenshitsukinuke-dōri no Koi'' (Kyoto's Love on Tenshitsukinuke Street).
* {{ill|Masashi Yamada (director)|ja|山田雅史|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Masashi Yamada (director)|1=Masafumi Yamada|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11470732}} - A film director who produced ''Tenshitsukinuke Rokuchōme.''
